summary: This is a brief, somber post from August 31, 2005, written in the immediate aftermath of Hurricane Katrina's devastation of New Orleans. Joel shares a photo from a happier visit to the city in 2003 and quotes from the Book of Lamentations. The post is a moment of pause from his usual software commentary to acknowledge a national tragedy. It reflects the shock and grief felt across the country as the scale of the disaster became clear.
key_points: Joel pauses his regular software commentary to acknowledge the devastation of Hurricane Katrina in New Orleans
He shares a personal connection to the city through his 2003 visit
The biblical quote from Lamentations 1:1 draws a parallel between ancient and modern urban destruction
money_quotes: How doth the city sit solitary, that was full of people!
key_insight: A rare non-technical post where Joel steps away from software commentary to mourn the destruction of New Orleans by Hurricane Katrina, using a biblical lament to capture the tragedy.
